Issue: Add support for connected component-based Dice metric calculation to the existing DiceMetric and DiceHelper classes
Decision: Add per_component parameter to DiceMetric and DiceHelper classes
Rationale: To compute Dice scores for each connected component individually, enabling more granular evaluation of segmentation quality. This addresses the need for per-component analysis in medical imaging, as referenced in https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.18684 and https://github.com/alexanderjaus/CC-Metrics
Author: VijayVignesh1
Description: DiceMetric and DiceHelper classes in MONAI's Project-MONAI/MONAI repository
Cost: Implementation complexity for connected component-based Dice metric calculation, including Voronoi region computation and per-component Dice scoring
Risk: Potential technical debt from adding new methods (compute_cc_dice, compute_voronoi_regions_fast) and dependencies (SciPy/cuPy for Voronoi regions). Also, input validation requirements (5D binary segmentation with 2 channels) may introduce constraints for users.

Argument.author: aymuos15
Argument.argument: Would be nice to extend this to both 2d and 3d within this PR itself.

Argument.author: aymuos15
Argument.argument: MONAI also entertains cupy, e.g: `https://github.com/Project-MONAI/MONAI/blob/dev/monai/data/image_reader.py` so having the option to do the cc step through that would be cleaner in gpus.

Argument.author: VijayVignesh1
Argument.argument: I've added both 2d capabilities as well as cupy option.
